messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] course on CRISPR  for Ecology and Evolution Studies (13–16 Oct, online)
messages.subject_clean: course on CRISPR  for Ecology and Evolution Studies (13–16 Oct, online)
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
 
There?s still time to register for the Physalia course on  Introduction to CRISPR  for Ecology and Evolution Studies (13?16 Oct, online)! 
 
In this course, participants will learn how to design and implement CRISPR-Cas9 gene knockout (KO) experiments. Through a mix of lectures and project-based hands-on activities, the course will provide both theoretical foundations and practical guidance for successful Cas9-mediated KOs in an evolutionary context.
 
 
For more details: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course53/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course53/ )
 
Best regards,
 
Carlo
 

--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] online course: RAD-Seq Data Analysis
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
We would like to announce that only a few seats remain available for upcoming Physalia online course:
RAD-Seq Data Analysis.
 
 Dates: 27?31 October

Course. website:  [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course16/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course16/ ) 
 
 
 
This five-day course will provide both conceptual background and hands-on training using Stacks for the analysis of Reduced representation genome sequencing data  (RAD-seq, ddRAD, 2bRAD, GBS, etc.). We will cover the entire workflow: from data preprocessing and quality assessment, to loci assembly (de novo and reference-based), marker selection, and applications in population genetics, association studies, and phylogenetics.
 
The course combines lectures, discussions, and practical coding sessions, with ample opportunity to discuss and work on your own data.
 
 
For the full list of our courses and workshops, please visit:  [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/ )[  ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course63/ )
 
 
 
Best regards,
Carlo
 
 
--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Genomics for Ecological and Evolutionary Studies - last chance
messages.subject_clean: Genomics for Ecological and Evolutionary Studies - last chance
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Last chance to enroll in our 5-day seminar on Genomics for Ecological and Evolutionary Studies <https://instats.org/seminar/genomics-for-ecological-and-evolutionary>, running livestreaming Oct 13 ? 17 and led by professors Giulio Formenti from the Vertebrate Genome Lab at The Rockefeller University and Guido Gallo from the Department of Biosciences at the University of Milan. Designed for PhD students, academics, and professional researchers, this hands-on workshop blends foundational theory with practical bioinformatics, guiding you through the evolution of DNA sequencing from Sanger to next-generation and third-generation technologies. You?ll learn to process and analyze real sequencing data using command-line tools such as Bowtie 2, Samtools, Minimap2, Freebayes, and IGV, and you?ll gain experience assembling draft genomes, evaluating assembly statistics, and integrating genomic insights into ecological and evolutionary research questions.

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/genomics-for-ecological-and-evolutionary> to secure your spot, and feel free to share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
https://instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Course: Online.PopulationGenomicslcWGS.20–23 October
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
Only a few seats remain for the upcoming Physalia online course ?Population Genomic Inference from low-coverage Whole-Genome Sequencing data? (20?23 October).
 
Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course64/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course64/ )
 
This hands-on course will guide participants through the concepts, workflows, and tools (including ANGSD and related software) for analysing low-coverage genomic data ? from raw reads to population structure, demographic inference, and selection analyses.
 
If you work with population, evolutionary, or medical genomics data, this is your last chance to join us and gain practical experience in probabilistic approaches for low-coverage data analysis.
 
For the full list of our courses and workshops, please visit: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course64/ )
 
 
Best regards,
 
Carlo
 
 

--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Deep Learning with ChatGPT 2.0 - livestream seminar
messages.subject_clean: Deep Learning with ChatGPT 2.0 - livestream seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Instats is excited to offer a 1-day seminar, Deep Learning for Complex Datasets with ChatGPT 2.0 <https://instats.org/seminar/deep-learning-for-complex-datasets-with-2>, livestreaming on Oct 27th and led by Nikolay Oskolkov, Group Leader at LIOS and professor in Molecular Biosciences at Lund University. Harnessing the synergy between state-of-the-art neural networks and Large Language Models, this intensive workshop will show you how to build, train, and evaluate deep learning models that reveal hidden patterns in complex biological, medical, and high-dimensional datasets. Through hands-on coding in R, Python, TensorFlow, and Keras?augmented by ChatGPT as a real-time coding partner?you?ll explore convolutional and recurrent networks, autoencoders, Bayesian approaches, and cutting-edge applications in multi-omics, single-cell biology, and personalized medicine. By the end of the day you?ll be ready to integrate deep learning into your own research pipeline, confidently preprocess data, optimize hyperparameters, and critically assess model performance.

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/deep-learning-for-complex-datasets-with-2> to secure your spot, and feel free to share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
https://instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Analysis of DNA Methylation 2.0 - livestream seminar
messages.subject_clean: Analysis of DNA Methylation 2.0 - livestream seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Instats is excited to offer a 1-day seminar, Analysis of DNA Methylation 2.0 <https://instats.org/seminar/analysis-of-dna-methylation-20>, livestreaming on 27 October and led by Dr Aline Muyle from the Institute of Evolutionary Science of Montpellier (ISEM). With next-generation sequencing now enabling nucleotide-level, genome-wide insight even in non-model organisms, this workshop empowers researchers to tackle whole-genome bisulfite sequencing (WGBS) and long-read datasets from PacBio and Oxford Nanopore. Dr Muyle will guide you through end-to-end bioinformatic workflows she employs in her own research?covering quality control, alignment, methylation calling, differential methylation analysis, haplotype-specific inference, and links to gene expression and natural selection?while providing all scripts and example data so you can immediately adapt methods to your own projects. Designed for PhD students, faculty, and professional scientists in ecology and evolution, the course blends concise lectures with hands-on coding sessions to deliver both conceptual understanding and practical skills that translate directly to publication-ready results.

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/analysis-of-dna-methylation-20> to secure your spot, and please share with colleagues who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
https://instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] RAD-seq Data Analysis - online course, 27–31 October
messages.subject_clean: RAD-seq Data Analysis - online course, 27–31 October
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
 
This is the last call to join our online course ?RAD-seq Data Analysis,? taking place from 27?31 October.
 
The course introduces reduced representation genome sequencing methods and focuses on data analysis using #Stacks, covering all steps from short-read data to genome variant interpretation for population genetics, phylogenetics, and association studies.
 
The training combines lectures and hands-on practicals, ideal for researchers working with or planning to analyze RAD-seq, ddRAD, 2bRAD, or GBS data from non-model organisms.
 
 Don?t miss the opportunity to join this edition!
 
[ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course16/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course16/ )
 
 
Best regards,
Carlo
 

--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Generative AI for Data Analysis - livestream seminar
messages.subject_clean: Generative AI for Data Analysis - livestream seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Instats is excited to offer a 1-day seminar, Generative AI for Data Analysis <https://instats.org/seminar/generative-ai-for-data-analysis>, livestreaming October 30 and led by Dr Kimberly Fessel. Designed for postgrads, academics, and research professionals, this hands-on workshop shows how large language models such as ChatGPT can be integrated into Jupyter-driven Python workflows to streamline data cleaning, accelerate exploratory analysis, generate synthetic datasets, and iterate on visualizations?all while maintaining rigorous, reproducible research standards. Through short lectures and supervised coding sessions, you will learn prompt-engineering strategies, best practices for AI-assisted code, and practical techniques for documenting and auditing every step of your workflow, leaving the day with ready-to-use notebooks and templates that immediately enhance your research productivity.

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/generative-ai-for-data-analysis> to secure your spot, and feel free to share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
https://instats.org
